news.title: Haryana government sets June 30 deadline for owners to join online land sale scheme
news.para: Haryana government has set a June 30, 2026 deadline for landowners to join the e-Bhoomi initiative for urban development. This voluntary drive aims to procure 1.7 lakh acres across 71 towns, with 47,000 acres targeted in the first phase. The scheme ensures transparent, digital land purchase with direct payments to owners, facilitating new sector development and easing housing demand.

news.title: CAG conducting special audit of 101 cities to assess ease of living
news.para: India is evaluating the quality of life in 101 cities, striving to improve citizen experiences across various urban landscapes. Concurrently, the Comptroller and Auditor General is delving into transport and logistics systems. This initiative unfolds against the backdrop of the 5th BRICS Supreme Audit Institutions Leaders Summit in Bengaluru, which emphasizes the importance of urban mobility.

news.title: MHADA issues letter of acceptance to consortium for 33-acre Abhyudaya Nagar redevelopment
news.para: MHADA has accepted a consortium's bid for the cluster redevelopment of Mumbai's Abhyudaya Nagar colony. This significant project will provide residents with larger rehabilitation flats and includes revised terms like parking, a corpus fund, and rent compensation. The redevelopment aims to renew aging public housing under the C&D model.

news.title: Vertical property cards proposal back with Maharashtra government; cabinet nod likely in June
news.para: Maharashtra is set to introduce "vertical property cards" for flat owners, a revised proposal awaiting cabinet review in June. This system will provide independent records of rights for individual apartments, detailing ownership, area, and loans. Once approved, it aims to enhance property title clarity and reduce transaction risks.

news.title: Landowners & builders equally accountable for project delay: Karnataka-RERA
news.para: Karnataka-RERA rules landowners and developers equally accountable, dismissing complaint over delayed Bengaluru housing scheme and disputed financial management issues

news.title: ED raids 'aide' of Punjab CM's OSD, others in land 'fraud' case; CM denies involvement
news.para: The Enforcement Directorate conducted searches across Punjab and Chandigarh as part of a money laundering investigation into two real estate groups. Investigators seized approximately one crore rupees in cash, including money thrown from a high-rise building. The probe involves allegations of fraudulent land use changes and duping investors. Documents suggesting interference in government functioning were also recovered.

news.title: DDMA issues eviction notices to Yamuna Bazar residents, warns of demolition
news.para: Residents of Yamuna Bazar have received eviction notices, granting them a 15-day window to clear the area. Following that period, demolition will commence if compliance is not met. This proactive measure aims to enhance safety during the imminent monsoon floods, as unauthorized constructions on DDA floodplain land could endanger lives.

news.title: Zillow, Redfin fail to end FTC lawsuit claiming they suppressed rental competition
news.para: A federal judge has dealt a blow to Zillow and Redfin by denying their motion to dismiss a lawsuit filed by the U.S. Federal Trade Commission. The FTC asserts that these real estate giants have unlawfully conspired to curb competition in online apartment rental listings, raising concerns that such actions could drive up advertising prices for landlords.

news.title: Haryana RERA's Gurugram bench approves 35 projects in Jan-Apr 2026
news.para: Haryana RERA-Gurugram has approved 35 projects in the initial four months of 2026. Residential projects accounted for 23 of these approvals. The authority has also cleared all complaints filed up to 2024.

news.title: CBI files fifth chargesheet in builder-banker nexus cases against CHD Developers
news.para: The CBI has filed a fifth chargesheet against CHD Developers Ltd and its directors. This action stems from alleged fraudulent activities in a Gurugram-based project. The builder company is accused of luring homebuyers and investors with false assurances. This resulted in financial gains for the company and wrongful losses for others.

news.title: Bagmane Prime Office REIT IPO subscribed 23.71 times
news.para: Bagmane Prime Office REIT's initial public offer saw strong investor interest, closing with 23.71 times subscription. The Rs 3,405-crore IPO attracted over two lakh applications. Institutional investors subscribed 25.25 times, while other investors bid 21.87 times. The REIT secured Rs 1,150 crore from anchor investors. Proceeds will fund property acquisitions.

news.title: Chhattisgarh offers 50% rebate on property registration for women
news.para: Chhattisgarh has announced a 50% rebate on property registration fees for properties registered in women's names, aiming to boost their economic security and social standing. Chief Minister Vishnu Deo Sai hailed the move as a historic step for women's empowerment, expecting long-term benefits to outweigh the revenue impact.

news.title: Indiabulls Real Estate case: Four entities pay ₹10.5 crore to Sebi as settlement
news.para: Market regulator Sebi settled a fund diversion case against four entities linked to the former Indiabulls Real Estate Ltd. The companies paid a total of Rs 10.49 crore to close the proceedings. This case involved allegations of fund diversion from Albasta Infrastructure Ltd to promoter-connected entities. Sebi investigated potential misuse of funds between FY10 and FY17.

news.title: Arisinfra subsidiary secures ₹650 crore DaaS contract for Wadhwa’s Panvel township
news.para: Arisinfra Solutions’ subsidiary ArisUnitern RE Solutions has secured an eight-month service order from Wadhwa Construction & Infrastructure for its township project Wadhwa Wise City in Panvel. The mandate involves project execution and monetisation support for inventory linked to an estimated gross development value of about ₹650 crore.

news.title: Rising packaging bag costs major headwind for UltraTech Cement: Official
news.para: UltraTech Cement faces headwinds from soaring plastic packaging bag prices, with costs surging by approximately Rs 90 crore in the last quarter due to West Asia conflict-driven supply chain disruptions. The company, however, aims for double-digit volume growth in FY27, supported by its scale, diversified sourcing, and long-term fuel contracts.

news.title: HMG Stones to invest ₹40 crore in FY27, eyes ₹120-150 crore revenue
news.para: HMG Stones is planning to invest about ₹40 crore in FY27, including ₹30 crore in machinery and ₹10 crore in a new experience centre near Bengaluru airport, as it looks to expand its natural stone business. The company expects revenue to grow to ₹120-150 crore in FY27 from over ₹100 crore in FY26, even as logistics costs and import dependence remain key concerns.

news.title: Enforcement directorate raids builders in Punjab in land 'fraud' case
news.para: The Enforcement Directorate (ED) conducted searches across multiple locations in Punjab and Chandigarh as part of a money laundering investigation into an alleged land fraud case. The raids targeted builders and real estate companies involved in the Suntec City project, with allegations of fraud in obtaining land use licenses and collecting crores from investors.
